description: This figure presents two linearized Arrhenius-type plots used to determine the dynamic recrystallization (DRX) activation energy of low-density high-strength Fe-Mn-Al-C steel subjected to hot compression. Subplot (a) plots the natural logarithm of strain rate ln(ε̇) against ln[sinh(ασp)] for deformation temperatures ranging from 900°C to 1150°C (black: 900°C, red: 950°C, blue: 1000°C, magenta: 1050°C, green: 1100°C, dark blue: 1150°C), yielding an average slope k₁ = 5.135. Subplot (b) plots ln[sinh(ασp)] versus the reciprocal temperature 1000/T (K⁻¹) for strain rates of 0.01, 0.1, 1, and 10 s⁻¹, with an average slope k₂ = 15.690. According to the caption and citation text, these linear fittings were used to calculate the DRX activation energy as 669.844 kJ mol⁻¹. The observed lack of perfect parallelism among the fitted lines suggests that the deformation conditions significantly influence the activation energy, indicating that the calculated value represents an average within the experimental range.
